Japanese restaurant owners in China lament flare-up in diplomatic tensions
data.description:
SHANGHAI (Reuters) -Takashi Ito, owner of a restaurant in Shanghai, had been looking forward to China's lifting of a ban on imports of Japanese seafood - only to have his hopes dashed this week amid an increasingly heated diplomatic spat between Beijing and Tokyo.

Tensions between the Asian neighbours flared up after new Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said this month that a Chinese attack on Taiwan threatening Japan's survival could trigger a military response.

China, which regards the democratically governed island as its own, responded with ire. In addition to reinstating a ban on Japanese marine produce, it has boycotted travel to Japan, and threatened stern countermeasures. Numerous meetings and cultural events have also been cancelled.

"Every time a major incident like this happens, we are in great pain because our hearts rise and fall as the relationship between Japan and China rattles every time. It's very painful," Ito told Reuters at his Japanese seafood restaurant Merase.

In perhaps a worrying sign, the restaurant also had a few cancellations on Wednesday night, although the potential customers didn't say why. Chinese customers generally account for half of Merase's bookings.

Beijing had only recently partially eased restrictions on Japanese seafood that had been imposed due to Tokyo's decision two years ago to release treated wastewater from its Fukushima power plant, the site of a 2011 nuclear meltdown that followed a massive earthquake and tsunami.

Ito has tried to source locally as much as possible but some types of fish can only be procured from Japan.

"This incident is one of the biggest incidents so far," he said, adding that he believes it's unlikely there will be any good news about seafood imports soon.

"Takaichi said what she said and I don't think she will change it. If possible, I hope diplomats in Japan and China can work together to improve the situation. I hope that the people of China and Japan will be able to enjoy the same foods without fighting each other," he said.

The dispute has seen vitriolic responses by a Chinese diplomat in Japan and Chinese state media aimed at Takaichi. That's prompted Japan to warn its citizens in China to step up safety precautions and avoid crowded places.

But both Ito and fellow Shanghai-based restaurateur Kazuaki Sone say they are not concerned about their own safety - only about the potential that problems between governments will make it harder for people to continue connecting for business, food or cultural exchange.

"I've lived a long time in China and I have experienced diplomatic tensions between Japan and China several times," said Sone who moved to China in 2012 and opened a yakitori restaurant, Hyakumanben, two years ago.

"But people in general, especially good friends, don't treat me any differently ... That's why I am still able to work here in China," he said.

(Reporting by Casey Hall and Go Nakamura; Additional reporting by Nicoco Chan in Shanghai; Editing by Edwina Gibbs)

By Casey Hall and Go Nakamura

BNP Paribas: Market cheers as 2027 financial strength target raised
data.description:
On Thursday BNP Paribas announced that it has raised its Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital ratio target to 13% by 2027, in addition to launching a €1.15bn share buyback program, two moves that were applauded by investors on the Paris stock exchange this morning.

At around 9:30 a.m., the stock was up 5.6%, making it the top performer on the CAC 40 and one of the strongest risers on the pan-European STOXX Europe 600 index. In comparison, the STOXX Europe 600 Banks sector index was up just 1.4% at the same time.

In a statement released early this morning, the eurozone's second-largest bank by market capitalization said it now aims for a financial strength ratio of 13% by 2027, up from the 12.5% target announced at its Q3 results presentation last October.

For context, the consensus was expecting 12.8% for this timeframe.

The bank explained that it intends to reach this goal through a combination of three levers: (1) improving profitability with a positive impact on organic capital generation, (2) moderate growth in risk-weighted assets estimated at around 2% per year, and (3) accelerating the disposal of non-strategic assets.

The group also confirmed its forecast for a return on tangible equity (ROTE)--a key measure of profitability--of 13% in 2028, representing a 2.1 percentage point increase compared to 2024.

To achieve this, BNP Paribas plans to capitalize on strategic initiatives already underway in several business lines, maintain disciplined growth focused on operational efficiency, while keeping a tight control over costs.

At the same time, the bank plans to offer shareholders an "attractive and disciplined" distribution policy, which will include the launch this month of a €1.15bn share buyback program, anticipating the distribution of 2025 results, as authorized by the European Central Bank.

In its statement, BNP Paribas said that its growth and profitability trajectory through 2028 will be detailed upon the publication of its FY 2025 results early next year, noting that these announcements lay the groundwork for its 2027-2030 strategic plan, to be unveiled at the start of 2027.

In a reaction note, analysts at Jefferies welcomed these measures, describing them as a genuine "inflection point" likely to change investor perception.

"In our view, this is a clear demonstration of the group's strength," the American broker wrote, which maintains a "buy" rating on the stock with a target price of €95.

Oddo BHF analysts also reiterated their "outperform" rating on the stock following these announcements.

The broker still believes that the stock is trading at very low multiples and that uncertainty relating to the Sudan litigation is more than priced in at current levels.

Global Markets Lifted by Nvidia's Record Earnings
data.description:
By Dow Jones Newswires Staff


Nvidia's record earnings lifted U.S. stock futures and international equities, while the U.S. dollar rose as investors dialed back expectations of another Federal Reserve interest rate cut next month.

Tech shares in particular gained after Nvidia reported record sales after the closing bell and Chief Executive Jensen Huang's guidance soothed fears over the sustainability of the AI-led boom that had dented investors' appetite in prior sessions. Huang said sales of Nvidia's Blackwell superchips were "off the charts" and those of its graphics processing chips in the cloud were sold out. Its shares gained as much as 6.5% after hours, boosting segment stocks in Asia and Europe Thursday and contributing to a return of risk appetite.

Meanwhile, the U.S. dollar gained after the Bureau of Labor Statistics said it won't release a full U.S. jobs report for October, instead pushing it to be published along with a full report for November, after the Federal Reserve next meets.


--S&P 500 futures were recently up 1.3%, futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.6% while those tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq were up nearly 1.8%.

--European chip stocks rose in morning trade on Nvidia's tailwind; Dutch semiconductor-equipment maker ASML jumped 3%, while smaller peers BE Semiconductor Industries and ASM International traded up 4.8% and 3.2%, respectively. Infineon Technologies climbed 3%, while STMicroelectronics gained 1.8%. The pan-European Stoxx Europe 600 gained 0.9% at the open and the U.K.'s FTSE 100 was up 0.7%.

--Asian indexes closed mostly higher, supported mainly by technology stocks. South Korea's Kospi closed up 1.9%, while Japan's Nikkei was up 2.7%. Still, mainland China shares gave up their early morning gains, while the Hang Seng ended flat.

--The U.S. dollar rose against a basket of major currencies. The Bureau of Labor Statistics' decision to release just some October U.S. nonfarm payrolls data after the Fed's December decision "reduces the data availability for the Fed before the meeting and thus makes a [decision to hold rates] more likely," Deutsche Bank analysts wrote. The minutes of the October Fed meeting released Wednesday also showed many officials leaned against a December rate cut. The DXY dollar was last up 0.1% to 100.284 after reaching a two-week high of 100.321 earlier.

--U.S. Treasury yields edged higher after the Fed minutes showed there were "strongly differing views" about the committee's policy decision in December. The 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point to 4.140%, according to Tradeweb data. Eurozone bond yields tracked Treasury yields higher. U.K. government bond yields, or gilts, eased after the latest British Retail Consortium Consumer Confidence survey showed deterioration in consumer sentiment, raising the prospects of a Bank of England rate cut. Gilt yields had jumped late on Wednesday amid some investor concerns over next week's budget.

--Bitcoin recovered slightly after Nvidia's earnings boosted appetite for risky assets. "It's fair to say that Nvidia's results have completely changed the market mood and pushed out any [AI] bubble fears for another day," Deutsche Bank analysts said in a note. Bitcoin was recently up 1.9% to $92,255, according to LSEG data, having plumbed a seven-month low of $88,526 on Wednesday.

--U.S. crude stocks fell by 3.4 million barrels against expectations of a 100,000-barrel rise, while gasoline inventories fell on lower weekly consumption. "Going forward, markets will focus on how sanctions reshape trade flows, whether supply growth continues to outpace demand, and the extent to which geopolitical risks inject fresh volatility into prices," MUFG's Soojin Kim says.

--In commodities, gold prices slipped on the slightly stronger U.S. dollar; futures in New York were down 0.8% to $4,052.80 a troy ounce. Oil prices rose in early trading in Europe after settling 2% lower in the previous session as investors weighed risks to Russian supplies and a mixed U.S. inventory report. Brent crude and WTI both rose 0.5% to $63.83 and $59.56 a barrel, respectively.


Write to Barcelona editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-20-25 0439ET

NetEase's Profit Growth Accelerates on Game Strength
data.description:
By Sherry Qin


Chinese videogame giant NetEase reported stronger quarterly profit growth, driven by the strong performance of established titles during the summer break, indicating a clear recovery momentum in its key game business.

The company behind popular games such as "Fantasy Westward Journey" and "Marvel Rivals" on Thursday reported a 32% jump in net profit to 8.62 billion yuan, equivalent to $1.21 billion. Analysts had expected 8.825 billion yuan, according to a FactSet consensus estimate. Adjusted net profit, a closely watched metric that excludes share-based compensation expenses, rose 27% from a year earlier, slightly ahead of estimates.

Revenue at the Hangzhou-based company climbed 8.2% to 28.36 billion yuan, missing market expectations slightly. Sales from games and value-added services rose to 23.3 billion yuan, driven by self-developed games such as "Eggy Party" and "Where Winds Meet."

The solid third-quarter performance comes as NetEase continues to capitalize on its established games, rolling out regular updates and expansions and strengthening operations. More than 20 years after its launch, the original PC version of "Fantasy Westward Journey" broke its own concurrent-user record earlier this month, surpassing 3.58 million gamers after the game maker launched a new server in the summer.

NetEase's robust game pipeline is expected to continue supporting its top line. Citi analysts highlighted that it has two more game launches in the typically soft fourth quarter, following four launches in October.

The game maker's next most-anticipated title in 2026 is "Ananta," a free-to-play role-playing game. It received an overwhelming response from the gaming community following the live demo conducted in September, the analysts said in a note.

NetEase's Hong Kong-listed shares have rebounded by more than 50% this year, bolstered by improved market sentiment toward the videogame industry amid a more stable regulatory environment. Investors have also rewarded the company for its diverse and expanding game portfolio, which has boosted user engagement and spending per user despite macroeconomic uncertainties.

Last week, NetEase's larger rival Tencent reported resilient results, with its international games revenue surging 43% in the third quarter.


Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-20-25 0431ET

Bayer Receives Accelerated U.S. Approval for Lung Cancer Drug
data.description:
BERLIN (dpa-AFX) - The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted Bayer accelerated approval for a lung cancer medication. Hyrnuo, which contains the active ingredient Sevabertinib, has been approved for the treatment of previously treated patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who have been found to carry mutations in the HER2 protein, the pharmaceutical and agrochemical group announced on Thursday.

According to the company, the accelerated approval is based on the objective response rate (ORR) and duration of response (DOR) observed in the ongoing Phase I/II SOHO-01 study. In this trial, the drug was administered to patients with advanced NSCLC and a HER2-activating mutation, whose disease continued to progress despite at least one systemic therapy.

Globally, it is estimated that up to 84,000 people are diagnosed each year with HER2-mutated NSCLC, the statement continued. According to Bayer, these patients generally have a poor prognosis and limited treatment options. "The FDA approval of Sevabertinib opens up a new therapeutic option," study lead Xiuning Le was quoted as saying in the release.

Whether Bayer will also receive permanent approval for Sevabertinib for this indication will likely depend on the review and demonstration of clinical benefit in a confirmatory study, the company added. Such trials are typically required following an accelerated approval./mis/tav/nas

data.description:
BERLIN (Reuters) -German union Verdi said on Thursday it has agreed with Lufthansa's Eurowings business on salary increases and other bonuses for 1,800 flight attendants.

Verdi said Eurowings' cabin crew had agree to a pay increase of a total of 5.4 per cent in two stages.

(Writing by Madeline ChambersEditing by Ludwig Burger)

AstraZeneca gets US approval for Koselugo for rare genetic disorder
data.description:
(Alliance News) - AstraZeneca PLC on Thursday said US regulators have approved its drug Koselugo for use in adults with neurofibromatosis type 1, expanding access to a treatment already used in children with the rare genetic condition.

The Cambridge, England-based pharmaceutical company said the US Food & Drug Administration granted approval based on results from Komet, the first and only placebo-controlled phase 3 trial in adults with NF1 and inoperable plexiform neurofibromas.

Neurofibromatosis type 1 is a lifelong, progressive condition caused by mutations in the NF1 gene. Up to half of patients develop plexiform neurofibromas, which are non-cancerous tumours that can grow along nerves and become debilitating.

The study showed a 20% overall response rate in tumour shrinkage for patients treated with Koselugo, compared with 5% for those on placebo.

AstraZeneca said 86% of responders maintained tumour reduction for at least six months. Safety results were consistent with the medicines established profile in paediatric use.

Marc Dunoyer, chief executive officer of Alexion, AstraZenecas rare disease unit, said the approval "enables much-needed continuity of care" for patients as they age, following the recent US approval of a granule formulation for children aged one and above.

Koselugo for adults has also recently secured approval in the EU and Japan, with additional regulatory reviews ongoing.

AstraZeneca shares were up 1.2% at 13,762.00 pence in London on Thursday morning.

By Eva Castanedo, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News Ltd. All Rights Reserved.

data.title: Halma plc Reports Earnings Results for the Half Year Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Halma plc reported earnings results for the half year ended September 30, 2025. For the half year, the company reported sales was GBP 1,237.4 million compared to GBP 1,074.3 million a year ago. Net income was GBP 186.8 million compared to GBP 136.2 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was GBP 0.4944 compared to GBP 0.3608 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was GBP 0.4929 compared to GBP 0.3598 a year ago.

data.title: Auterion says Rheinmetall stake to boost drone software group's growth
data.description:
DUESSELDORF, Germany (Reuters) -Auterion CEO Lorenz Meier said having German defence player Rheinmetall on board as a "significant" shareholder in the U.S.-based drone software firm would help it grow its order book, adding that it would not lead to a full takeover.

Auterion already provides software for some Rheinmetall drones and the direct investment, which was announced this week, is a way to strengthen collaboration, Meier told Reuters.

He did not disclose the exact size of Rheinmetall's stake, which while "significant", was not a blocking minority.

"We're incredibly pleased with this honour, especially in the German market," said Meier, adding that closer ties between the two firms could help Auterion win more contracts.

STRONG COLLABORATION, NOT TAKEOVER

Rheinmetall CEO Armin Papperger told analysts on Tuesday that the defence firm had bought Auterion shares, without giving any additional details. 

Meier said Rheinmetall, which sees mergers and acquisitions as key to rapidly expanding its business to meet demand, is not aiming for a takeover with the move and Auterion is capable of remaining independent for another 30 years.

Auterion is currently exploring the market for more software functions to acquire, he added.

ORDER BOOKS EXPECTED TO KEEP GROWING

Rheinmetall and Auterion, which signed a long-term cooperation agreement in December 2024, are working together on drone components with the goal of creating an industry standard for controlling and operating them in various environments.

Auterion was founded in 2018 and became profitable this year. It has annual revenue of around 100 million euros ($115 million) and 160 employees, both of which Meier expects to grow.

Meier said Auterion is providing software for the drones built by Rheinmetall to fulfil orders for loitering munitions, which hover over targets before striking. 

Auterion, whose customers already include the U.S. and Ukraine, as well as the Netherlands, Britain and Taiwan, expects to win an order from Germany after demonstrating a drone swarm control system three months ago, Meier said.    

Rheinmetall plans to restructure from next year to reflect changed priorities, including a new air defence unit with mid-term sales potential of 3 billion euros to 4 billion euros.

Its CEO Papperger said demand is mainly being driven by European interest in the face of repeated drone incursions.

($1 = 0.8680 euros)

(Reporting by Matthias Inverardi and Miranda Murray; Editing by Alexander Smith)

By Matthias Inverardi and Miranda Murray
